data.title: Integrate Semgrep with mandatory security rulesets into CI pipeline template
data.description:
Integrate Semgrep static analysis into the CI pipeline template generated by /dso:onboarding, with mandatory rulesets and government-safe configuration.

## Mandatory Rulesets

The following rulesets must run on every PR:

| Ruleset | Coverage |
|---------|----------|
| p/security-audit | Baseline security checks |
| p/secrets | Hardcoded credentials, API keys, tokens |
| p/owasp-top-ten | OWASP Top 10 vulnerability patterns |
| p/cwe-top-25 | CWE/SANS Top 25 dangerous software errors |
| Trail of Bits community rules | trailofbits/semgrep-rules — Python, Go, Ruby, JS/TS, Terraform |

Additional language-specific rulesets activated based on project stack detection:
- Python: p/python + p/django / p/flask / p/fastapi (as applicable)
- JavaScript/TypeScript: p/javascript + p/react / p/express / p/nextjs
- Go: p/golang
- Java: p/java + p/spring
- Infrastructure: p/terraform / p/kubernetes / p/cloudformation / p/dockerfile / p/github-actions

## Critical: Government Configuration

**--metrics=off is mandatory on every Semgrep invocation.** Without this flag, code contents are transmitted to Semgrep's telemetry servers. This is unacceptable for government codebases subject to FISMA, FedRAMP, and data sovereignty requirements.

## Integration Points

1. **CI pipeline template** — Add Semgrep scan step to the CI workflow template generated by /dso:onboarding (examples/ci.example.yml)
2. **Pre-commit hook** (optional, configurable) — Run p/secrets locally before commit to catch credentials early
3. **Code review overlay** — Semgrep findings should feed into the security red-team review overlay as deterministic input (similar to how the ACCESSIBILITY_REVIEWER agent consumes Axe Core output)
4. **dso-config.conf** — Add configuration keys:
   - semgrep.enabled=true/false
   - semgrep.rulesets (comma-separated, defaults to mandatory set above)
   - semgrep.metrics=off (locked for government, configurable for non-gov)
   - semgrep.fail_on=error (block merge on high-severity findings)
   - semgrep.pro=false (Semgrep Pro cross-file taint tracking catches ~250% more true positives but requires license)
5. **Stack detection** — /dso:onboarding should auto-select language-specific rulesets based on detect-stack.sh output

## Reference

Trail of Bits security skills (trailofbits/skills on GitHub) document that Semgrep Pro cross-file taint tracking catches ~250% more true positives than OSS mode for inter-file data flows. Consider recommending Pro for government projects handling sensitive data.
